rag_rules: For validator diagnostics about type mismatches, align the helper declaration type with the expression. Comparison expressions are bool; integer literals and integer arithmetic are int; real literals and real arithmetic are real.

For validator diagnostics about unresolved identifiers or invalid guarantees, trace the first unresolved identifier or invalid helper declaration on the same line. Fix the root unresolved reference, or delete the invalid clause instead of adding architecture.
For validator diagnostics about invalid scalar type names, use AGREE scalar declaration types: int, real, and bool.
For validator diagnostics about mismatched input types in the 'prev' function, ensure the first and second arguments are of the same type.
For validator diagnostics about incompatible classifiers, ensure the types of the identifiers match.
generic_rules: Use only visible AADL features or eq helpers declared in the same annex.
Ensure the first and second arguments of the 'prev' function are of the same type.
Ensure the types of the identifiers match when they are used in the same context.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE scalar declaration types are int, real, and bool.
Comparison expressions are bool; integer literals and integer arithmetic are int; real literals and real arithmetic are real.
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in a component type annex.
Do not add architecture to fix invalid guarantees; instead, delete the invalid clause.
Do not use invalid scalar type names in AGREE declarations.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
